cues.text: Take three slow, settling breaths, in through the nose, out through the mouth.

Let go of any agenda for these next few minutes.
There's no right answer. There's no performance here.

Think of your body, something it did for you today without being asked.
Your lungs breathed.

Just notice that.
Let yourself feel a small flicker of appreciation.

Now think of one person in your life who makes things a little easier, a little warmer.
Someone close... or someone you barely know.
A kind stranger. A good friend.

Hold them in your mind for a moment.
Feel that warmth toward them.

Now think of something small that you actually enjoy.
The first sip of something hot.
Sunlight through a window.
A sound you love.
Something easy... and true.
Let yourself enjoy it, right now, just in remembering.

Notice where you feel gratitude in your body.
Some people feel it in their chest, an opening... a warmth.
Let it settle there.

You don't have to be grateful for everything.
You don't have to pretend life is perfect.
But right now, in this breath,
there is something good.
When you open your eyes, carry one of these moments with you, into the next hour.

The good is already here.
You just looked at it.
